meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Fix Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing work. Learn to implement a robust SaaS starter kit to solve version control issues using Antigravity and Claude Code for faster client delivery.
content.answer_block: Implementing a standardized SaaS starter kit eliminates 'no version control' issues by embedding Git workflows, automated CI/CD, and modular architecture from day one. By leveraging Antigravity for structural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refactoring, you ensure every project is version-controlled, professional, and audit-ready, significantly reducing technical debt for your clients.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, a client demands a feature revert, and you’re staring at a chaotic folder of 'final_v2_real.zip' files. The lack of version control isn't just a technical oversight; it’s a ticking time bomb that erodes trust and kills your professional velocity. When every project starts from scratch, you aren't building a business—you're playing digital archeology.

It’s time to move to a factory model. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you transform your workflow from reactive chaos to proactive engineering. We will leverage Antigravity to generate high-fidelity boilerplate and Claude Code to maintain rigorous versioning standards, ensuring your code remains clean, traceable, and ready for production at a moment's notice.

steps.title: Initialize the Starter Foundation
steps.description: Establish a base repository that enforces strict commit conventions and directory structure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade SaaS starter kit template including Next.js, Tailwind, Supabase, and pre-configured Husky hooks for commit linting.

steps.title: Automate Branching Logic
steps.description: Create a standardized Git workflow that separates feature development from production releases.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that enforces branch protection rules and requires status checks for any merge into the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create .github/workflows/main.yml with branch protection automation'

steps.title: Implement Semantic Versioning
steps.description: Ensure every update is tracked with clear, machine-readable version tags.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate semantic-release into the repository to automate version numbering and changelog generation based on conventional commits.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'install and configure semantic-release for npm'

steps.title: Sanitize Environment Variables
steps.description: Prevent credential leakage by enforcing a standardized .env.example pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a script that validates the existence of all required environment variables against a .env.example file at build time.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add dotenv-vault for secure environment management'

steps.title: Finalize CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Connect your repository to a deployment provider for automated, version-controlled shipping.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a deployment script for Vercel/Railway that only triggers on successful build and test completion.

pro_tips: Always use Conventional Commits (e.g., feat:, fix:, chore:) to allow automated tools to parse your project history.
Never commit dependencies directly; use lockfiles (package-lock.json/yarn.lock) to ensure environment parity across all client machines.
Implement a 'dry-run' CI step in your starter kit to catch linting errors before they hit your production branch.
Use git hooks to automatically run prettier and eslint on every commit, saving you hours of manual cleanup.
Keep your starter kit repo as a template repository on GitHub so you can spin up new client projects in under 30 seconds.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/book
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a private GitHub template repository. Use this as your 'Golden Image' for every new client project to ensure consistent version control from day one.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping like a pro. Standardizing your workflow is the single biggest lever for scaling your freelance income. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
